raw.objective: To examine “the ability to detect a pharmacokinetic interaction between clonazepam and carbamazepine on the applicability and performance of pharmacokinetic screening using a population approach”
raw.covariates: Tested: ? Included: WT, comedication of CBZ on CL, WT on Vd
raw.observations: Simplified PK model only characterises Css, which does not allow absorption/distribution phases.
raw.dose: Steady state oral doses in patients
raw.equations: CL=theta1*(WT**theta2)*(theta3**CBZ)*(1+eta1) Cssipred=D/(CL*Tau) Cssobs=Cssipred*(1+eps1) Css=steady state concentrations D=CLNZ dose Tau=dosing interval CBZ= comedication with CBZ (1=yes, 0=no)

extraction_target.title: Pharmacoepidemiologic investigation of a clonazepam-carbamazepine interaction by mixed effect modeling using routine clinical pharmacokinetic data in Japanese patients.

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL=theta1*(WT**theta2)*(theta3**CBZ)*(1+eta1) Cssipred=D/(CL*Tau) Cssobs=Cssipred*(1+eps1) Css=steady state concentrations D=CLNZ dose Tau=dosing interval CBZ= comedication with CBZ (1=yes, 0=no)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady state oral doses in patients
study.objective: To examine “the ability to detect a pharmacokinetic interaction between clonazepam and carbamazepine on the applicability and performance of pharmacokinetic screening using a population approach”

covariates.raw: Tested: ? Included: WT, comedication of CBZ on CL, WT on Vd

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
